title: Rust graph fusion passes for attention patterns
description:
Implement two new graph optimization passes in synapse-graph crate.

1. `synapse/crates/synapse-graph/src/fuse_attention.rs`: FuseAttention pass.
   - Detects pattern: QKV projection → split heads → matmul → scale → softmax → matmul → concat → output projection
   - Replaces with single FusedAttention node
   - Add `OpKind::FusedAttention` variant to the IR enum in ir.rs
   - Implement `OptimizationPass` trait: `fn name() -> &str` returns "fuse_attention", `fn run(graph) -> Result<bool>` performs the pattern match and replacement

2. `synapse/crates/synapse-graph/src/fuse_layernorm_residual.rs`: FuseLayerNormResidual pass.
   - Detects pattern: Add(x, residual) → LayerNorm
   - Replaces with single FusedLayerNormResidual node
   - Add `OpKind::FusedLayerNormResidual` variant to IR enum
   - Implement `OptimizationPass` trait

Also add `OpKind::LayerNorm` to the IR if not present (needed for the fusion pattern).

Register both new passes in the optimization pipeline (update lib.rs or pipeline registration).

Tests:
- FuseAttention: construct a graph with the attention pattern, run pass, verify pattern is replaced with single node. Verify node count reduction.
- FuseLayerNormResidual: construct graph with Add → LayerNorm, run pass, verify fusion. Verify node count reduction.
- Passes are idempotent: running twice produces same result.
- Existing DCE, constant folding, and Phase 1 fusion passes still work correctly.

Pass/fail:
- Both passes correctly detect and fuse their patterns
- New OpKind variants added to IR
- Passes registered in pipeline
- Existing graph tests still pass (no regressions)
